description: NTS_RM_CN Algorithm Input Example - Response Matrix with Crank-Nicolson for 2D Neutron Transport

metadata.method_type: Response Matrix Method with Crank-Nicolson Time Integration
metadata.file_type: Plain text input (converted to JSON for reference)

program_overview.name: NTS_RM_CN.c
program_overview.purpose: Solves 2D neutron transport equation using Response Matrix method with Crank-Nicolson scheme
program_overview.method_description: Combines Response Matrix formulation with Crank-Nicolson implicit time stepping for high accuracy
program_overview.advantages: Very accurate, unconditionally stable, excellent for steady-state problems
program_overview.computational_cost: Higher than DD or LD, but provides highest accuracy
program_overview.input_parameters: Identical to NTS_DD and NTS_LD (11 parameters)
program_overview.output_format: JSON with STATUS, ITER, CPU, MFLUX, MFLOW, XFLOW, YFLOW

example_1_simple_problem.name: Simple 2×2 Domain - RM_CN Method
example_1_simple_problem.description: Simple domain 20×20 cm, one material zone, uniform sources, vacuum boundaries
example_1_simple_problem.physics: Tests RM_CN accuracy with simple geometry - highest precision expected

json_format.accuracy_level: Very high - Response Matrix method is most accurate

expected_output.convergence_pattern: Very fast - RM_CN converges in few iterations
expected_output.accuracy: Highest among DD, LD, and RM_CN

example_2_heterogeneous_materials.name: Heterogeneous Problem with Multiple Materials
example_2_heterogeneous_materials.description: Two materials in checkerboard pattern - tests RM_CN with material interfaces

json_format.note: RM_CN handles material interfaces with high precision

example_3_fine_mesh_high_precision.name: Fine Mesh with High Precision Requirements
example_3_fine_mesh_high_precision.description: Finer discretization for maximum accuracy verification
example_3_fine_mesh_high_precision.physics: Tests RM_CN with refined mesh - ideal application

json_format.spatial: 3 regions in both X and Y (non-uniform)
json_format.accuracy_expected: Highest - RM_CN with fine mesh and strict tolerance

example_4_asymmetric_sources.name: Problem with Asymmetric Sources and Boundaries
example_4_asymmetric_sources.description: Tests RM_CN with complex source distribution

json_format.note: RM_CN maintains stability with asymmetric problems

parameter_definitions.note: Input parameters identical to NTS_DD and NTS_LD (11 parameters)

NZ.name: Number of Material Zones
NZ.recommendation: RM_CN handles multiple zones very efficiently

NR_X, XDOM.name: X Domain Discretization
NR_X, XDOM.recommendation: 5-10 nodes per region optimal for RM_CN

NR_Y, YDOM.name: Y Domain Discretization
NR_Y, YDOM.recommendation: 5-10 nodes per region optimal for RM_CN

ZMAP.name: Zone Map
ZMAP.format: NR_Y × NR_X matrix with indices 1 to NZ

QMAP.name: Source Map
QMAP.format: NR_Y × NR_X matrix with source values (≥ 0)

TOL.name: Convergence Tolerance
TOL.note: RM_CN can achieve tighter tolerance efficiently

method_explanation.response_matrix: Precomputes transfer operators between region boundaries for rapid convergence
method_explanation.crank_nicolson: Implicit time-stepping scheme provides unconditional stability
method_explanation.combination: RM + CN = high accuracy + fast convergence + stability
method_explanation.internal_spectrum: Computes eigenvalues/eigenvectors (spectral analysis) for each zone

output_fields.CPU: Execution time (usually fastest of the three methods)
